findings.detail: The new release commands can tag and publish from the wrong commit after the PR merge. The recipe runs `gh pr merge` while the local checkout remains on `chore/release-<version>`, then the release section immediately shows `git tag v<version> && git push origin v<version>`. With `--merge`, the tag may miss the actual merge commit; with the documented `--squash` or `--rebase` alternatives, it can point at a branch commit that is not the protected branch result at all. Add an explicit post-merge sync/verification step before R1/R2, such as fetching/switching to `main`, fast-forwarding from `origin/main`, confirming post-merge CI green, and tagging/publishing from that checked-out protected-branch commit, or explicitly tag `origin/main`.

next_action: Revise `docs/RELEASING.md` so the post-merge release steps operate on the verified protected-branch commit, then rerun the phase review.
